versions.note: Near-perfect — and that's the warning sign, not the win

inProgress: v2 — the C-NMC 2019 dataset, which ships patient IDs, so cells from one patient can be kept entirely within one split. The honest accuracy there will almost certainly be lower — and more trustworthy.

changed: First end-to-end build: a YOLO11s-cls network fine-tuned to sort blood-smear white cells into 4 classes — Benign plus three B-cell ALL maturation stages (Early Pre-B, Pre-B, Pro-B).
Reuses the MoleCheck computer-vision pipeline (transfer learning → ONNX for the browser, TFLite for the app).
Exported to ONNX (21 MB) running on-device in the browser demo.
improved: Learns the four cell types cleanly: 99.8% accuracy and 0.998 macro-F1 on the held-out images.
knownGaps: That 99.8% is a red flag, not a triumph. This dataset ships no patient IDs, so a random split almost certainly puts the same patient's cells in both training and test — the model can key on patient/staining artifacts instead of leukemia morphology. v2 (C-NMC, which has patient IDs) does the rigorous patient-level split; expect a lower, more honest number there.
